If the GET parameter user_field_names is provided and its value is one of the following: `y`, `yes`, `true`, `t`, `on`, `1`, or empty, then the key will instead be the actual name of the field.

field_23.description: This field represents the `multiple_collaborators` field. The number in field_23 is in a normal request or response the id of the field. If the GET parameter user_field_names is provided and its value is one of the following: `y`, `yes`, `true`, `t`, `on`, `1`, or empty, then the key will instead be the actual name of the field. This field accepts a list of objects representing the chosen collaborators through the object's `id` property. The id is Baserow user id. The response objects also contains the collaborator name directly along with its id.

field_26.description: This field represents the `password` field. The number in field_26 is in a normal request or response the id of the field. If the GET parameter user_field_names is provided and its value is one of the following: `y`, `yes`, `true`, `t`, `on`, `1`, or empty, then the key will instead be the actual name of the field. Allows setting a write only password value. Providing a string will set the password, `null` will unset it, `true` will be ignored. The response will respond with `true` is a password is set, but will never expose the password itself.

field_28.description: This field represents the `ai` field. The number in field_28 is in a normal request or response the id of the field. If the GET parameter user_field_names is provided and its value is one of the following: `y`, `yes`, `true`, `t`, `on`, `1`, or empty, then the key will instead be the actual name of the field. Holds a value that is generated by a generative AI model using a dynamic prompt.

permissions.description:
Indicates per operation which permissions the database token has within the whole workspace. If the value of for example `create` is `true`, then the token can create rows in all tables related to the workspace. If a list is provided with for example `[["table", 1]]` then the token only has create permissions for the table with id 1. Same goes for if a database references is provided. `[['database', 1]]` means create permissions for all tables in the database with id 1.

Example:
```json
{
  "create": true// Allows creating rows in all tables.
  // Allows reading rows from database 1 and table 10.
  "read": [["database", 1], ["table", 10]],
  "update": false  // Denies updating rows in all tables.
  "delete": []  // Denies deleting rows in all tables.
 }
```

If no scopes are provided then all actions performed in the client session will be included when undoing/redoing.

additionalProperties.description: An object containing error messages by fields. The key is the field name and the value is an array of error messages for this field. These messages are localized for the user who has created the job when the translation is available.

failing_rows.description: An object containing field in error by rows. The keys are the row indexes from original file and values are objects of errors by fields.

type.description:
The type of the job.

* `duplicate_application` - duplicate_application
* `install_template` - install_template
* `create_snapshot` - create_snapshot
* `restore_snapshot` - restore_snapshot
* `export_applications` - export_applications
* `import_applications` - import_applications
* `airtable` - airtable
* `duplicate_table` - duplicate_table
* `duplicate_field` - duplicate_field
* `sync_data_sync_table` - sync_data_sync_table
* `duplicate_page` - duplicate_page
* `publish_domain` - publish_domain
* `duplicate_automation_workflow` - duplicate_automation_workflow
* `publish_automation_workflow` - publish_automation_workflow
* `generate_ai_values` - generate_ai_values
* `audit_log_export` - audit_log_export
* `file_import` - file_import
The type of the job.

progress_percentage.description: A percentage indicating how far along the job is. 100 means that it's finished.

state.description: Indicates the state of the import job.

human_readable_error.description: A human readable error message indicating what went wrong.

RoleTypeEnum.description:
* `allow_all` - Allow All
* `allow_all_except` - Allow All Except
* `disallow_all_except` - Disallow All Except

db_index.description: If true, then an index will be added to the Baserow field to increase lookup and filter speed. Note that this comes at a performance cost when creating the row and updating the cell.

duration_format.description:
The format of the duration.

* `h:mm` - hours:minutes
* `h:mm:ss` - hours:minutes:seconds
* `h:mm:ss.s` - hours:minutes:seconds:deciseconds
* `h:mm:ss.ss` - hours:minutes:seconds:centiseconds
* `h:mm:ss.sss` - hours:minutes:seconds:milliseconds
* `d h` - days:hours
* `d h:mm` - days:hours:minutes
* `d h:mm:ss` - days:hours:minutes:seconds
* `d h mm` - days:hours:minutes:with_spaces
* `d h mm ss` - days:hours:minutes:seconds:with_spaces

number_suffix.description: The suffix to use for the field.

date_show_tzinfo.description: Indicates if the time zone should be shown.

date_include_time.description: Indicates if the field also includes a time.

number_separator.description:
The thousand and decimal separator to use for the field.

* `` - No formatting
* `SPACE_COMMA` - Space, comma
* `SPACE_PERIOD` - Space, period
* `COMMA_PERIOD` - Comma, period
* `PERIOD_COMMA` - Period, comma

date_force_timezone.description: Force a timezone for the field overriding user profile settings.

number_prefix.description: The prefix to use for the field.

through_field_id.description: The id of the link row field to rollup values for.

target_field_id.description: The id of the field in the table linked to by the through_field to rollup.

rollup_function.description: The rollup formula function that must be applied.

type.description: The type of the related field.

primary.description: Indicates if the field is a primary field. If `true` the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

read_only.description: Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.

immutable_type.description: Indicates whether the field type is immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the field type via the API.

immutable_properties.description: Indicates whether the field properties are immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the properties and the type via the API.

database_id.description: The ID of the database this field belongs to.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace this field belongs to.

* `` - No formatting
* `SPACE_COMMA` - Space, comma
* `SPACE_PERIOD` - Space, period
* `COMMA_PERIOD` - Comma, period
* `PERIOD_COMMA` - Period, comma

date_force_timezone.description: Force a timezone for the field overriding user profile settings.

number_prefix.description: The prefix to use for the field.

through_field_id.description: The id of the link row field to rollup values for.

target_field_id.description: The id of the field in the table linked to by the through_field to rollup.

rollup_function.description: The rollup formula function that must be applied.

db_index.description: If true, then an index will be added to the Baserow field to increase lookup and filter speed. Note that this comes at a performance cost when creating the row and updating the cell.

duration_format.description:
The format of the duration.

* `h:mm` - hours:minutes
* `h:mm:ss` - hours:minutes:seconds
* `h:mm:ss.s` - hours:minutes:seconds:deciseconds
* `h:mm:ss.ss` - hours:minutes:seconds:centiseconds
* `h:mm:ss.sss` - hours:minutes:seconds:milliseconds
* `d h` - days:hours
* `d h:mm` - days:hours:minutes
* `d h:mm:ss` - days:hours:minutes:seconds
* `d h mm` - days:hours:minutes:with_spaces
* `d h mm ss` - days:hours:minutes:seconds:with_spaces

number_suffix.description: The suffix to use for the field.

date_show_tzinfo.description: Indicates if the time zone should be shown.

date_include_time.description: Indicates if the field also includes a time.

number_separator.description:

* `` - No formatting
* `SPACE_COMMA` - Space, comma
* `SPACE_PERIOD` - Space, period

* `PERIOD_COMMA` - Period, comma

user_id.description: The user who made the comment.

table_id.description: The table the row this comment is for is found in.

row_id.description: The id of the row the comment is for.

mode.description:
The mode to use to receive notifications for new comments on a table row.

* `all` - all
* `mentions` - mentions

action_type.description: The type of the action that was performed.

action_command_type.description:
The type of command that was performed.

* `DO` - DO
* `UNDO` - UNDO
* `REDO` - REDO

user.description: The user that performed the action.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the action that was performed.

before.description: The mapping between field_ids and values for the row before the action was performed.

after.description: The mapping between field_ids and values for the row after the action was performed.

fields_metadata.description: The metadata of the fields that were changed.

name.description: The first name of the user.

row_comment_count.description: How many row comments exist for this row.

field_id.description: An id of a select field of the table. The value provider return the color of the selected option for each row.

new_email.description: The new email address to change to.

password.description: The current password of the user for verification.

base_url.description: The base URL where the user can confirm the email change. The confirmation token is going to be appended to the base_url (base_url '/token').

email.description: The email address of the user that has requested a password reset.

base_url.description: The base URL where the user can reset his password. The reset token is going to be appended to the base_url (base_url '/token').

username.description: Required. 150 characters or fewer. Letters, digits and @/./+/-/_ only.

allow_new_signups.description: Indicates whether new users can create a new account when signing up.

allow_signups_via_workspace_invitations.description: Indicates whether invited users can create an account when signing up, even if allow_new_signups is disabled.

allow_reset_password.description: Indicates whether users can request a password reset link.

allow_global_workspace_creation.description: Indicates whether all users can create workspaces, or just staff.

account_deletion_grace_delay.description: Number of days after the last login for an account pending deletion to be deleted

show_admin_signup_page.description: Indicates that there are no admin users in the database yet, so in the frontend the signup form will be shown instead of the login page.

track_workspace_usage.description: Runs a job once per day which calculates per workspace row counts and file storage usage, displayed on the admin workspace page.

show_baserow_help_request.description: Indicates whether the `We need your help!` message will be shown on the dashboard

co_branding_logo.description: Co-branding logo that's placed next to the Baserow logo (176x29).

email_verification.description:
Controls whether user email addresses have to be verified.

* `no_verification` - no_verification
* `recommended` - recommended
* `enforced` - enforced

verify_import_signature.description: Indicates whether the signature of imported files should be verified.

db_index.description: If true, then an index will be added to the Baserow field to increase lookup and filter speed. Note that this comes at a performance cost when creating the row and updating the cell.

type.description: The type of the related field.

primary.description: Indicates if the field is a primary field. If `true` the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

read_only.description: Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.

immutable_type.description: Indicates whether the field type is immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the field type via the API.

immutable_properties.description: Indicates whether the field properties are immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the properties and the type via the API.

database_id.description: The ID of the database this field belongs to.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace this field belongs to.

data.description:
A list of rows that needs to be created as initial table data. Each row is a list of values that are going to be added in the new table in the same order as provided.

Ex: 
```json
[
  ["row1_field1_value", "row1_field2_value"],
  ["row2_field1_value", "row2_field2_value"],
]
```
for creating a two rows table with two fields.

If not provided, some example data is going to be created.

first_row_header.description: Indicates if the first provided row is the header. If true the field names are going to be the values of the first row. Otherwise they will be called "Field N"

data.description:
A list of rows you want to add to the specified table. Each row is a list of values, one for each **writable** field. The field values must be ordered according to the field order in the table. All values must be compatible with the corresponding field type.

Ex: 
```json
[
  ["row1_field1_value", "row1_field2_value"],
  ["row2_field1_value", "row2_field2_value"],
]
```
for adding two rows to a table with two writable fields.

upsert_values.description:
A list of values that are identifying rows in imported data.
 The number of rows in `upsert_values` should be equal to the number of rows in imported data. Each row in `upsert_values` should contain a list of values that match the number and field types of fields selected in `upsert_fields`. Based on `upsert_fields`, a similar upsert values will be calculated for each row in the table.
 There's no guarantee of uniqueness of row identification calculated from `upsert_values` nor from the table. Repeated upsert values are compared in order with matching values in the table. The imported data must be in the same order as the table rows for correct matching.

skipped_fields.description: A list of field IDs that should not be overwritten during upsert operations.

event_config.description: A list containing the addition event options.

headers.description: The additional headers as an object where the key is the name and the value the value.

calls.description: All the calls that this webhook made.

use_user_field_names.description: Indicates whether the field names must be used as payload key instead of the id.

url.description: The URL that must be called when the webhook is triggered.

event_id.description: Event ID where the call originated from.

db_index.description: If true, then an index will be added to the Baserow field to increase lookup and filter speed. Note that this comes at a performance cost when creating the row and updating the cell.

text_default.description: If set, this value is going to be added every time a new row created.

type.description: The type of the related field.

primary.description: Indicates if the field is a primary field. If `true` the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

read_only.description: Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.

immutable_type.description: Indicates whether the field type is immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the field type via the API.

immutable_properties.description: Indicates whether the field properties are immutable. If true, then it won't be possible to change the properties and the type via the API.

name.description: The human readable name of the database token for the user.

workspace.description: Only the tables of the workspace can be accessed.

key.description: The unique token key that can be used to authorize for the table row endpoints.

permissions.description:
Indicates per operation which permissions the database token has within the whole workspace. If the value of for example `create` is `true`, then the token can create rows in all tables related to the workspace. If a list is provided with for example `[["table", 1]]` then the token only has create permissions for the table with id 1. Same goes for if a database references is provided. `[['database', 1]]` means create permissions for all tables in the database with id 1.

Example:
```json
{
  "create": true// Allows creating rows in all tables.
  // Allows reading rows from database 1 and table 10.
  "read": [["database", 1], ["table", 10]],
  "update": false  // Denies updating rows in all tables.
  "delete": []  // Denies deleting rows in all tables.
 }
```

anyOf.description: Indicating if the database token has permissions to all tables.

Second element should indicate the ID of the reference.

action_type.description: If an action was undone/redone/skipped due to an error this field will contain the type of the action that was undone/redone.

action_scope.description: If an action was undone/redone/skipped due to an error this field will contain the scope of the action that was undone/redone.

result_code.description: Indicates the result of the undo/redo operation. Will be 'SUCCESS' on success, 'NOTHING_TO_DO' when there is no action to undo/redo and 'SKIPPED_DUE_TO_ERROR' when the undo/redo failed due to a conflict or error and was skipped over.

role.description:
The role required to update the data for this field.

* `ADMIN` - Admin
* `BUILDER` - Builder
* `EDITOR` - Editor
* `NOBODY` - Nobody

allow_in_forms.description: Whether to allow this field to be shown in forms. Default is False. This setting is only relevant if the role is not 'EDITOR'.

field_id.description: The ID of the field whose permissions were updated.

can_write_values.description: Whether the user can write values to this field.

username.description: Required. 150 characters or fewer. Letters, digits and @/./+/-/_ only.

UserAdminCreate.description:
Serializes a request body for creating a new user. Do not use for returning user
data as the password will be returned also.

is_active.title: Active
is_active.description: Designates whether this user should be treated as active. Set this to false instead of deleting accounts.

is_staff.title: Staff status
is_staff.description: Designates whether this user is an admin and has access to all workspaces and Baserow's admin areas.

UserAdminResponse.description: Serializes the safe user attributes to expose for a response back to the user.

is_active.title: Active
is_active.description: Designates whether this user should be treated as active. Set this to false instead of deleting accounts.

is_staff.title: Staff status

permissions.description: The permissions that the user has within the workspace.

UserSourceUser.description: A serializer used to serialize a UserSourceUser object.

UserWorkspaceInvitation.description:
This serializer is used for displaying the invitation to the user that doesn't
have access to the workspace yet, so not for invitation management purposes.

email.description: The email address of the user that the invitation is meant for. Only a user with that email address can accept it.

message.description: An optional message that the invitor can provide. This will be visible to the receiver of the invitation.

UsersPerUserSource.description: The response of the list user source users endpoint.

users_per_user_sources.description: An object keyed by the id of the user source and the value being the list of users for this user source.

filter_type.description:
Indicates whether all the rows should apply to all filters (AND) or to any filter (OR).

* `AND` - And
* `OR` - Or

filters_disabled.description: Allows users to see results unfiltered while still keeping the filters saved for the view.

public_view_has_password.description:
Indicates whether the public view is password protected or not.

:return: True if the public view is password protected, False otherwise.

allow_public_export.description: Indicates whether it's allowed to export a publicly shared view.

view.description: The view to which the decoration applies. Each view can have his own decorations.

type.description: The decorator type. This is then interpreted by the frontend to display the decoration.

value_provider_type.description: The value provider type that gives the value to the decorator.

value_provider_conf.description: The configuration consumed by the value provider.

order.description: The position of the decorator has within the view, lowest first. If there is another decorator with the same order value then the decorator with the lowest id must be shown first.

view.description: The view to which the filter applies. Each view can have his own filters.

field.description: The field of which the value must be compared to the filter value.

type.description: Indicates how the field's value must be compared to the filter's value. The filter is always in this order `field` `type` `value` (example: `field_1` `contains` `Test`).

value.description: The filter value that must be compared to the field's value.

preload_values.description: Can contain unique preloaded values per filter. This is for example used by the `link_row_has` filter to communicate the display name if a value is provided.

group.description: The id of the filter group this filter belongs to. If this is null, the filter is not part of a filter group.

filter_type.description:
Indicates whether all the rows should apply to all filters (AND) or to any filter (OR) in the group to be shown.

* `AND` - And
* `OR` - Or

view.description: The view to which the filter group applies to. Each view can have its own filter groups.
The view to which the group by applies. Each view can have his own group bys.

field.description: The field that must be grouped by.

order.description:
Indicates the sort order direction. ASC (Ascending) is from A to Z and DESC (Descending) is from Z to A.

* `ASC` - Ascending
* `DESC` - Descending

width.description: The pixel width of the group by in the related view.

type.description: Indicates the sort type. Will automatically fall back to `default` if incompatible with field type.

view.description: The view to which the sort applies. Each view can have his own sortings.

field.description: The field that must be sorted on.

Indicates the sort order direction. ASC (Ascending) is from A to Z and DESC (Descending) is from Z to A.

* `ASC` - Ascending
* `DESC` - Descending

type.description: Indicates the sort type. Will automatically fall back to `default` if incompatible with field type.

permissions.description: The permissions that the user has within the workspace.

workspace.description: The workspace that the user will get access to once the invitation is accepted.

email.description: The email address of the user that the invitation is meant for. Only a user with that email address can accept it.

permissions.description: The permissions that the user is going to get within the workspace after accepting the invitation.

message.description: An optional message that the invitor can provide. This will be visible to the receiver of the invitation.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available for pagination

name.description: User defined name.

workspace.description: The workspace that the user has access to.

permissions.description: The permissions that the user has within the workspace.

user_id.description: The user that has access to the workspace.

to_be_deleted.description: True if user account is pending deletion.

id.description: The unique identifier for this team.

name.description: The team name that this workspace user belongs to.

WorkspaceUserWorkspace.description:
This serializers includes relevant fields of the Workspace model, but also
some WorkspaceUser specific fields related to the workspace user relation.

Additionally, the list of users are included for each workspace.

name.description: Workspace name.

order.description: The requesting user's order within the workspace users.

permissions.description: The requesting user's permissions for the workspace.

unread_notifications_count.description: The number of unread notifications for the requesting user.

generative_ai_models_enabled.description: Generative AI models available in this workspace.

button_hover_text_color.description: The text color of buttons when hovered

button_hover_border_color.description: The border color of buttons when hovered

button_active_background_color.description: The background color of buttons when active

button_active_text_color.description: The text color of buttons when active

button_active_border_color.description: The border color of buttons when active
